In this lesson, we're going to talk about the elements of the Six Figure Funnel Formula Customer Captivation Offer. Taking a peek at our funnel diagram, you can see that the Customer Captivation Offer is the second page in your funnel. This is the page as we've already covered that prospects come to after they enter their information into your lead magnet offer page. This is the second page in your funnel. Let's talk about what a Customer Captivation Offer is. A Customer Captivation Offer is a superior high value, low barrier offer designed to attract quality customers and transform them into raving fans. Let's break this down a little bit. Number one, it is superior. Superior to what? Well, as we've already talked about in Module 1, it's superior to the offers that are currently out in the marketplace. It's superior to competitors' offers. It's high value because it provides stellar quality content deep in a particular subject area. Low barrier offer because it is on the lower priced side. It's designed to attract quality customers because it's not a rock bottom pricing. It's not a $7 report or $9 report or $12 report. We're going to go over the pricing structure in just a bit. It's designed to transform them into raving fans because of the fact that you're giving such a stellar, over-the-top superior offer and then you're delivering on that offer. Let's go over the different elements of a Customer Captivation Offer. Number one, as you already know, you're seeing the common theme here that the offer is built on your hook and promise. You already know that on the lead magnet offer page, what gets offered is now what we're going to be talking about on your Customer Captivation Offer page. We're going to cover this in great detail in an upcoming lesson, how you actually do this and how you present your message. The Customer Captivation Offer is built off of the back of your hook and promise. It's based on your hook and promise. The Customer Captivation Offer is narrow in focus and scope. What that means is that you're not trying to deliver a wide variety of information about your particular topic. A Customer Captivation Offer needs to be narrow in scope. The reason why it needs to be narrow in scope is because a wide scope offer that is cheap gives prospects the feeling that there's no depth to the product. For example, if you talked about how to work out to lose weight in your first product, in your Customer Captivation product, that's too general, too wide at this price point for prospects to believe that there's any depth and quality to the subject because they know that if you are selling it at a low price point, it's either not that good or it's not that deep. So, Customer Captivation Offer is narrow in focus and scope. In terms of, let's say, using this example of working out to lose weight, we would pick a particular area of that subject. For example, maybe it is the cardio portion of your exercise program to lose weight. Again, it's narrow in scope and focus. Maybe it's on the abdominal training portion of the routine to lose weight. It needs to be narrow in focus and scope. The price point ranges between $19 and $29. You'll understand more about why this is so critical later on when we get to the transaction maximization sequence. Your Customer Captivation Offer is priced between $19 and $29. Meaning, it's not one of these $7, $9 or $12 offers that attracts low quality customers. This is low enough so that it's low barrier of entry, but high enough so that it is weeding out the really low value customers. Your Customer Captivation Offer also offers a lopsided value proposition. That just means that what you are offering, what you're going to deliver, what you're going to give to them compared to the $19 to $29 price point is lopsided in their favor in terms of value. In other words, if you were offering an e-book for $29, that's certainly not a lopsided value proposition. If, for example, your Customer Captivation Offer included a, let's say if you were selling an information product, one or two videos plus an audio version plus the transcript plus a little fill in the blank exercise sheet and it was $29, well now the value of the deliverables exceeds the price point. Not to mention the value of what they're going to learn in the Customer Captivation Offer or really your front end product is also lopsided in their favor. Your Customer Captivation Offer has one or more high value premiums or what is commonly referred to as bonuses. Bonuses can often drive the sale. The key with the bonuses is we're going to talk about in just a little bit is that they're high value. They're not thrown together. They're not last minute add-ons, but that they are high value. They are valuable enough so that a prospect would purchase the bonus alone if it was offered separately. Your Customer Captivation Offer needs to have one or more of these high value premiums or bonuses. Then finally the last element is that it's got a superior risk reversal component. This is at its most basic level is your guarantee, your money back guarantee. It's superior because we want to, as we talked about in Module 1, look at the different offers that are out there in the competitive landscape. Look at the risk reversal component, the guarantee and you want yours to be a bolder, bigger, better guarantee, a bigger, bolder, better risk reversal statement. Those are the elements of your Six Figure Funnel Formula Customer Captivation Offer. Just remember when it comes to this offer, more so than any other offer that we're going to talk about in the entire Six Figure Funnel Formula that this is key. It is the second most important element as we've talked about of your entire marketing funnel. Something else in the marketing funnel is nearly as important as this one offer. It does not matter how good the offers are in the transaction maximization sequence that we're going to talk about, the strategic upsell, downsell sequence that we're going to talk about. None of those things matter if a prospect doesn't take this first offer. This offer needs to be a stellar, over the top superior irresistible offer.
